reviews.review_description: Guys I’ve been doing graffiti since I was 14 and I’m 33. The only way to make sense out of this overall rating is that people using this are either new to murals and walls or technologically find it hard to jump on a new app. Within 5m from installing I had the picture low opacity, straighten on my wall perfectly adjusted waiting to get traced. It’s honestly amazing! Great job to the team!!

This app has been a game changer for me as an artist doing murals and woodcut pieces. This is the entire reason I bought my Meta Quest.  Not every wall I paint on has a space that can work for a projector, or that I can access when it is dark - so having the headset to get my outlines removes those barriers. 

Functionality wise, I agree with everyone who finds this confusing on first use. I too was really frustrated why the "preview screen" never went away. However, it is not supposed to go away. This is a panel you can grab by the horizontal bar beneath it and move spatially wherever you want (i.e. not right in front of your drawing surface) and this is your control panel while you are using the app.

Due to its non-intuitive usability and required learning curve I would probably give this app a 4 or 4.5 but I'm doing a 5 to balance what I feel were some unfair 1 star reviews. Also the developer (I'm pretty sure there is only one dude) was actually quite nice and responsive when I had a question once.

Once I figured it out (and it really only took one session to "get" it) I haven't had issues since.

My criticisms: I, too, wish it had a good memory and I could come back to a piece the next day. I also wish the drawing wouldn't somewhat "shift" spatially if it's on a large wall but in those cases I can do slight corrections manually if needed. And the system to get your file to it is a bit clunky. What I do is place my image (best results PNG with transparent bg) in a google drive, open browser app to download that, then it is in my files to open within Contour App.

That all said I don't think these are all issues this developer can overcome with the way the Meta Quest works and I think some of these reviews are harsh and ignorant about their criticisms. The app is functionally likely as good as it CAN be with the hardware constraints. 

I have done dozens of murals and art pieces using it and so have many of my artist friends, I will keep recommending it!

Worked for me under certain angles. It’s completely unusable when you’re sitting in front of a wall and you have to trace something that is near the floor. Tried ist also on a big facade and the image looked warped and it was moving. The artwork moves when you look up and down. I‘m frustrated and I‘m uninstalling it. I  Can’t used it

developer_response.body: Hi there, I'm a bit surprised with your feedback since thousands of users, including many professional muralists and artists, are using the app including for very large artwork. It's not perfect, but it should definitely not result in such a frustrating experience. Don't hesitate to reach out to the support email to tell me more.

reviews.review_description: I seen a bunch of videos so I downloaded it but to get your personal image from your phone to the app is a real struggle if you don’t have a laptop. So I wasn’t able to do it. Hopefully, they upgrade the app.

developer_response.body: Hi there, sorry you got frustrated, and indeed, I really wish Meta made it easier to import files to their headset. But you do not need a laptop to import images :  the simplest way is to email the images to yourself and use the Quest browser to download the images. This is what most users do. Don't hesitate to reach out to the support email if you need more  information, I'll be glad to help.

developer_response.body: Hi there and thanks for your feedback - The image import process should be pretty smooth, so I would love to understand which import you've been using specifically, and what happened. Can you please reach out by email so we can learn more ? Thanks !

reviews.review_title: I'm hopeful that this app'lll get better
reviews.review_description:
The tiktok users make it look much better than I have found it to be.  I think this app is a game changer to make murals, etc easier to 'prep' on a wall (sketch). But... I find it still is 'glitchy' when I use it. The picture I'm using moves... and so, I have to use my art skills, but then that's using up time that I was trying to save, by purchasing the Quest in the first place. 
I have spent a lot of money but I don't feel that this has been as good as those other guys make it out to be. 
I also think the process to get a file into the app is clunky: I have to upload to google drive, then use the quest browser to open up gdrive, download it to quest, open the contour app, upload it there... 
I have tried another app to see if it's better, but it's about the same.
I'm very grateful for this app, im a chalk artist and this app makes it easy and fun to get any design on concrete, wall you name it! 🙏

reviews.review_title: A Game Changer for Professional Woodwork
reviews.review_description: As a professional chainsaw carver, getting my proportions right on large-scale logs is critical. I’ve been using Contour for my templating, and it has streamlined my workflow significantly. It makes the transition from a 2D concept to a physical template much faster and more accurate. If you’re a maker or artist who needs reliable scaling, this app is a must-have tool in your digital kit.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, I'm sorry to read you had trouble with importing images.
It's hard to understand what was your specific issue from your message, but those problems are usually fairly easy to sort out. 
First, make sure to visit our website for detailed instructions, help and troubleshooting: https://visible-space.com/contour
In particular, make sure you have accepted the app permissions (Library / Hover Contour (three dots) / Settings) as they reset with each app updates. And if none of this worked, please reach out to dehats+contour@gmail.com so we can sort this out together.
Thank you

reviews.review_description: Anchoring the image to a place doesn't work properly, when I take off the goggles it changes the position of my project

developer_response.body: Hi, there is an option to save and load your image position precisely to avoid this can of trouble. Did you give it a try ? If needed, please don't hesitate to reach out at dehats+contour@gmail.com so we can have a closer look at your issue. Thanks !

reviews.review_title: Please Bring Back Flicker in Adjust Mode
reviews.review_description:
I’ve loved using Contour for my VR drawing workflow, especially the ability to project and align reference images. Before the recent update, you could flicker the image on and off with the “A” button while in the “Adjust” menu—this was absolutely essential for perfect alignment. Now, that flicker function is only available in the “Draw” menu, where you can’t move the image.

Being able to flicker and move the image at the same time made it possible to compensate for the occasional image drift or software hiccup. Without that option, alignment is much harder and feels less precise. I really hope the flicker function returns to the “Adjust” menu—it made a huge difference. Still love the app overall, and I know it’s growing!

You would think that uploading your original image to the app would be the most streamline and easy thing to do, but unfortunately it isn’t. This app makes me want to return the VR headset altogether.

developer_response.body:
Hi, sorry that you're having trouble with importing images, and I agree Meta could have made it easier to transfer images to the Quest.

But once you did, importing to Contour should be trivial. For more information on this process, I would strongly suggest visiting our website for detailed instructions, help and troubleshooting: https://visible-space.com/contour

And If you can't find an anwser to your problem, reach out to dehats+contour@gmail.com. I'm happy to help and we always manage to sort things out.

Shaped for incorporating corners etc…
I use this for my art business @paintedpanes and wowza. Total gem. Thank you devs for the hard work and continuous updates!

reviews.review_title: Great potential with some issues still
reviews.review_description:
Hi, is there a way to contact the developers? I have it working great and in mid paint it can either jump to somewhere else about a meter or two away from the pinned surface even at a new angle or it mover around 3/4 pixels every time I walk away from it for a few minutes? I would love to chat to someone to see if there is a better way to keep it fully locked. 

I am in draw mode when using it as directed…..
I love the application the only thing i would really like is a playlist option to listen to music while using it. That would really be perfect.

I gave it 5 stars so it be on top. This thing is a joke. It consistently moves When you’re tracing. The depth has no practical calibration to the actual surface you’re working on. Which makes the plane tilted no matter how hard you adjust. And when you adjust for one side the other is lifted. I’ve waisted time on this. A cheap projector is a better application for canvas than this. This is for children at best.

I've tried Contour with the Meta Quest 3 and I'm happy with the results in small and medium format. It remains to be seen how it works on walls that are really high...
I was able to solve the issue of incorporating images by saving them in square format to Google Drive and downloading them from the VR glasses. In my case, I do it in jpg (it doesn't have to be transparent since the program allows you to change the opacity).
However, I'm surprised that I can't control a couple of things that seem simple to me. I would like to be able to move the image more fluidly, in the same way that we can move the black menu by pressing the side button, and then adjust it with more precision. There are times when it can be good to have the image on the side of the mural, when we're already starting to paint it, as a visual reference. I also miss being able to rotate the image on an axis perpendicular to the image itself, since we don't always want a vertical image.
I hope they keep making improvements to this fantastic application!

This has made the app useless I’ve tried everything and apparently this isn’t just me either. I would love to use the app but it just refuses to upload images that are on my downloads on the quest. Yes it’s 1040 x 1040, yes it’s jpeg and also png. This is ridiculous please don’t waste your money

Maybe the app works well once it’s going, but it literally won’t even do anything besides get stuck at the opening preview window. So frustrating.

Don't bother with Side quest or developer mode etc for adding your photos to the quest. There's an easier way. Just use your Google photos account. Add your PNG files to your Google photos account. Then in meta quest bring up the browser and login to your Google photos account. Find the PNG you want and download it. Then in the Contour app go to the menu and the download folder and voilà there is your photo.  I would imagine this would work the same for Drop Box as well. 👍

A couple times more than the image got  out of alignment and or just randomly reconfigured itself to an entirely different plane. After all the methodic alignment the first time it was very hard to align a second time.

Its usable for sure, but looking forward to some updates.

Hi, sorry that you're having trouble with importing images, and I agree Meta could have made it easier to transfer images to the Quest.

reviews.review_title: Warning! Do not get this for Quest 2
reviews.review_description:
The idea for this app is great, but the pass through on the Quest 2 is so warped and grainy that you can't accurately see where your hand is, and it completely defeats the purpose. 

I will however provide you with solutions for the other 2 issues people seem to be experiencing. 

1) You can download images to your headset using something like Google Drive/Wetransfer etc, and download through the browser on your headset. 

2) If you don't want your image to stretch / warp, set it up on a square canvas of 1024px x 1024px or bigger. That means for portrait you'll have white columns on the side, and landscape top and bottom. They cannot be seen in your projection, and your image aspect ratio will remain true to the original.
It's a little glitchy, I email myself png then download to meta, they changed the scale option to use your remotes moving them both with the trigger but it doesn't work. Useless

Small improvements that would make a huge difference.
Photos/pictures uploaded have to be 1x1 or they skew during placement. Get to close as you draw and everything shifts.

you would think being 2025, VR headsets and all that, there would be a streamline way to share multi media from PC to Headset with just a couple mouse clicks... not so much, you have to jump through hoops to try and get an outside image on the headset for it to just not even work, ive saved images in countless places relating to the headset, yet nothing... Thanks

reviews.review_description:
Incredible software that enables the removal of light/laser protection methods from an artists workflow. I have introduced this into my process for most large intricate murals that are not a free flow graffiti style. 
I would guess on average cutting down my composition, layout and sketching time by half. 
Awesome, thanks for having the insight and putting in the dev time 🙏🏻👏🏻🙌🏻

this is the next level for any freehand artist and it works great... you need a computer and a link cable to put your images on it  not really at all a big deal .. send the images you make through the directory given on the app easy .. I have been doing custom air brushing for about 25 years and I would use a projector for highly detailed art .. but this app has almost made me put that stuff in the attic.. thank you and I will take any updates on this app it can only get better...

reviews.review_title: Awesome App! Now Easier Upload Options
reviews.review_description: Awesome for murals or any IRL art projects where proportions and sizing is important. Also recently got updated to include some easier image uploading options that don't require a computer 🥳.
I've been stuck on the loading screen for 30 minutes now for the last 2 days. Ask I have the quest 2 am I doing something wrong?

I purchased this app because of the ability to draw down an art piece in real life. However, when i tried, i did not feel comfortable because i couldn’t see what i was outlining, the picture kept moving, and it takes incredibly long to upload a reference photo.

Make it so you can upload from photos straight from the meta quest otherwise you have to go through this random folder that it’s so hard to find because there’s barely any information on the website and app.

This app is amazing! I did a mural using the app and it made things 10x faster for me. It locked my image in place and was super easy to adjust. Once you have it down, it will make a world of a difference to creat your large scale artwork.

reviews.review_title: Make it easier to upload pics
reviews.review_description: Pleas its so difficult to find the folder and to upload more art

reviews.review_title: Need alternative way to upload images
reviews.review_description:
Edit- 

1
Please allow us to load pics into any folder on the headset and preferablly throigh online or Bluetooth upload instead of cable.


2
Don't stretch the images into a square. This is such a frustration as it's not made clear anywhere so you spend all this time getting pics in right format and location and then they're unusable and you have to format into squares with gaps at sides

3
Please allow hand tracking mode so I can see my hands above the canvas/picture

4
Please provide a better anchor and positioning system. The image is usually going to need to be pinned to a canvas or paper which is at angle and not always the same angle on each side. It won't often be vertical or flat. This makes getting the alignment finicky and if it's not right the painting is totally squiffy which defeats the whole point of the app. It also moves slightly while you are drawing or painting and this knocks proportions out yet again.

5 
To make this usable you need to be able to save your positioning/setup/space to return to next time. Otherwise you have to go through the whole debacle again ans will never get the position exactly the same which yet again will make things squiffy
 
Overall I love what you are doing . It a just a few tweaks and you'll be totally flying. Keep going, don't give up and reach out if you want to work with me to get more feedback
I got images into the Quest 3 using Sidequest, but from there I see no way of getting them into the artwork folder.  Waiting on a response and will update.
App wouldn’t open up at all

The app is great and easy to use. Some limitations of not 100% accurate tracking is obviously not the fault of the app, but the issues around it are small.

What i would like to request is slightly more options for controlling the image positioning. I would suggest a variable setting for moving it up down, left right using the thumbstick, so that you can move it faster or slower. 

Also for positioning against the wall, especially for bigger projects (eg 6m x 6m), the ability to position it on the wall not just using the controller placed against the wall, but by using thumbsticks. 

Although thumbsticks do allow rotation around up axis, and forward/backward motion, I would be very grateful for clockwise/ anticlockwise rotation around the forward looking axis. 

And perhaps a small leveling type feature, that shows level vs actual orientation?

Thanks for the app, it is really great and usefull, and some small improvements will make it phenomenal!

When I open the image I added to the specified folder the scale automatically is adjusted to be square.  Also needs easier way to upload images instead of loading it through pc. This is very useful app for artists if it was developed more.

developer_response.body: Hi Ellion and thanks for taking the time to review Contour. You are correct, the virtual frame used by the app is always a square, which means if your original image has a different ratio, you have to make it a square by adding transparent padding. As for alternatives for loading images, the Quest OS unfortunately offers very few options to developers, and using a PC is the solution recommended by meta.

reviews.review_description: Does exactly what I hoped it would. Works well with full color pngs aswell. Just wondering if in the future it would be updated to quest 3 color passthrough, and maybe optimizing it for the mq3 controllers aswell. Love it so far, looking forward to see it evolve

reviews.review_title: Good but not supported on quest 3

The placement also says to put the rings on the ground but the Quest 3 controllers do not have rings like the Quest 2. Other than that amazing app!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review Jake - the app now supports the Quest 3, so I suggest you make sure to update the app to v.0.5.6 or above. However the artwork placement scheme still uses the red ring for now,  which I know isn't ideal. I'm still working on a better alternative for Quest 3 controllers.

reviews.review_description: this app could be amazing if the upload process was easier
